title: Covet thy gristle with vigor
description: A compact comic-prophetic artifact that turns bodily appetite and archaic command into a deliberately distorted utterance, preserving absurd vigor as signal rather than argument.

motifMap.meaning: The phrase turns the tough, bodily remnant into an object of exaggerated desire, making appetite feel both ridiculous and declarative.
The use of 'thy' gives the utterance mock-scriptural force, letting the sentence perform prophecy while refusing solemnity.
The artifact reads like a phrase translated through ritual, joke, and bodily instinct at once, preserving distortion as the point rather than an error.

associatedMedia.description: Rendered Substack post page for 'Covet thy gristle with vigor'. Screenshot capture was attempted during the first live v2.6.4 automation run but did not produce a local image file in this environment.
associatedMedia.altText: A minimal Substack post from Wanderer's Burn titled 'Covet thy gristle with vigor' by Unnamed, published April 23, 2026, containing the single line 'Covet thy gristle with vigor'.

editHistory.notes: First live v2.6.4 metadata automation trial. Extracted Substack title, slug, canonical URL, publication date, language, and body text from the live page. Classified the five-word artifact as Trickster Prophet with Fractured Translation variant due to its comic-prophetic command form, bodily absurdity, and deliberate language distortion.

integrity.notes: Filename: 2026-04-23.covet-thy-gristle-with-vigor.phase-trickster-prophet.variant-fractured-translation.metadata.v2.6.4.json. Screenshot capture remains the unresolved part of the first live run; page extraction and metadata generation completed from live Substack source.
